questions.question: Why does calling `.sort()` inside a `computed` mutate the original array?
options: Vue's reactivity system unwraps the array before passing it to the getter
`.sort()` modifies the array in place, so the computed getter and the source share the same mutated array
Computed getters run in a separate reactive context that merges back on completion
Vue clones all reactive values on read, causing the sorted version to replace the original

questions.question: Which ES2023 method is the non-mutating equivalent of `.sort()`?
Which of these correctly creates a sorted copy inside a computed without mutating the source?
options: `return items.value.map(i => i).sort((a, b) => a - b)` only
